meta.defaultDescription: Senior-led HubSpot strategy, implementation, CRM architecture, automation, integrations, websites, Service Hub, and managed support.

brand.positioning: A senior-led HubSpot consultancy that helps growing businesses design, build, connect, and improve the systems behind their customer journey.
brand.promise: Clear answers for complex HubSpot problems.
principles: Keep it as simple as it can be
Build for the people using it
Prefer reliable systems over clever demos
State what is known, assumed, and still needs testing

hero.headline: Your HubSpot answer.
hero.body: HubSpot should make your business clearer, not more complicated. 42 helps growing teams design, build, connect, and improve HubSpot around the way they actually work.

problem.eyebrow: The platform is not the problem
problem.headline: HubSpot is powerful. Your setup may not be.
problem.body: Most HubSpot problems are not caused by one missing workflow or dashboard. They come from disconnected decisions: unclear ownership, inconsistent data, processes that do not match the business, and tools that were never designed to work together.
items: A CRM the team does not trust
An implementation that stopped at setup
problem.closing: 42 finds the real problem, designs the right answer, and builds it into the platform.

difference.headline: Advice is useful. A working system is better.
difference.body: 42 works best with teams that want a thinking partner who can also open the portal, write the code, map the data, test the workflow, and explain what changed.
items: Get strategy and implementation in the same engagement
Build around the business process, not a generic template
Solve technical requirements beyond standard onboarding
Receive documentation, testing, training, and a clear handover
Keep the system understandable as it grows

steps.title: Understand
steps.body: Map the goals, teams, processes, data, constraints, and current platform.

steps.title: Architect
steps.body: Define the CRM model, system design, roadmap, responsibilities, and measures of success.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: Configure, develop, integrate, migrate, test, and document.

steps.title: Enable
steps.body: Train users, launch the system, monitor adoption, and improve what comes next.

integration.headline: When HubSpot needs to connect to the rest of the business.
integration.body: Native integrations are useful until the process becomes specific. 42 designs and builds the connection between HubSpot and the systems that handle finance, operations, service delivery, scheduling, quoting, commerce, and customer data.

booking.headline: Book a conversation with a HubSpot consultant.
booking.body: Bring the portal problem, integration requirement, process bottleneck, or project idea. You will leave with a clearer view of the problem and the most sensible next step.

engagements.name: Build
engagements.description: For clients who know change is required.

services.name: HubSpot strategy and consulting
services.headline: Make the right HubSpot decisions before building the wrong system.
services.summary: Map the business process, data, teams, constraints, and commercial goals before committing to configuration or development.
services.serviceLine: Make the right HubSpot decisions before investing in the wrong build.

services.name: Implementation and onboarding
services.headline: HubSpot implementation designed around your business.
services.summary: Move beyond default setup with a portal architecture, process design, migration plan, and launch that your teams can actually use.
services.serviceLine: A HubSpot implementation designed around the way your teams actually operate.

services.name: CRM and revenue operations architecture
services.headline: Turn customer data into a system your teams can trust.
services.summary: Create clear ownership, consistent data, usable pipelines, and reporting that supports decisions across the revenue journey.
services.serviceLine: Clean data, clear ownership, and processes that move revenue forward.

services.name: Automation and operations
services.headline: Automate the right work without creating a workflow maze.
services.summary: Design dependable automation that removes friction, protects data quality, and remains understandable as the business grows.
services.serviceLine: Automate the right work without creating a workflow maze.

services.name: Integrations and custom development
services.headline: Connect HubSpot to the systems that run your business.
services.summary: Design and build reliable integrations, middleware, custom objects, extensions, and modules when the native option does not fit.
services.serviceLine: When the native integration is not enough, build the right connection.

services.name: Websites and Content Hub
services.headline: Build a website that works as part of your HubSpot system.
services.summary: Connect content, conversion, CRM data, reporting, and customer journeys through a fast, flexible, well-designed web experience.
services.serviceLine: Websites built for HubSpot, not simply hosted on it.

services.name: Service Hub and customer experience
services.headline: Give service teams the full customer context.
services.summary: Build ticketing, Help Desk, routing, knowledge, automation, and reporting around the way customers actually need support.
services.serviceLine: Give service teams the context and systems to support customers properly.

services.name: Managed HubSpot support and optimisation
services.headline: Senior HubSpot capacity without another full-time hire.
services.summary: Keep improving the portal through a clear, prioritised backlog delivered by experienced consultants and technical specialists.
services.serviceLine: An experienced HubSpot team when you need answers, improvements, or extra capacity.

team.name: Thierry-Luc Denichaud
team.bio: Thierry-Luc leads 42's work across HubSpot strategy, CRM architecture, digital delivery, and hands-on problem solving, keeping complex programmes clear from first question to working system.
team.imageAlt: Portrait of Thierry-Luc Denichaud in a contemporary office

team.name: Luca Codevilla
team.bio: Luca focuses on turning business requirements into clear HubSpot architecture, practical workflows, and onboarding plans that teams can confidently adopt.
team.imageAlt: Portrait of Luca Codevilla in a contemporary office

team.name: Zane Smith
team.bio: Zane works across PHP development, APIs, data flows, and custom integrations, connecting HubSpot to the operational systems around it.
team.imageAlt: Portrait of Zane Smith in a contemporary office

team.name: Emma Black
team.bio: Emma connects marketing strategy with practical HubSpot onboarding, helping teams establish clear journeys, campaigns, content processes, and day-to-day ways of working.
team.imageAlt: Portrait of Emma Black in a contemporary office

insights.title: HubSpot pricing and AI credits, made simple

insights.imageAlt: Tiered software editions, seat tokens, and a finite AI credit ledger

faqs.question: What kind of HubSpot work does 42 handle?
faqs.answer: 42 supports strategy, implementation, CRM architecture, automation, integrations, custom development, websites, Service Hub, reporting, enablement, and managed optimisation.

faqs.question: Can 42 work with an existing HubSpot portal?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many engagements begin with an existing portal that needs to be reviewed, cleaned up, restructured, connected, or expanded.

faqs.question: Does 42 only provide recommendations?
faqs.answer: No. The consultancy combines discovery and solution design with hands-on configuration, development, testing, documentation, and enablement.

faqs.question: Can 42 build custom integrations?
faqs.answer: Yes. Integration work can include APIs, webhooks, middleware, custom objects, data synchronisation, custom-coded workflow actions, and architecture documentation.

faqs.question: Can 42 provide ongoing support?
faqs.answer: Yes. Managed support can provide prioritised delivery, portal administration, troubleshooting, optimisation, reporting improvements, and additional senior capacity.
